items.front: Three things prohibited in Class A airspace per 7-1-1
items.back: Visual separation, VFR clearances, and VFR-on-top clearances.

items.front: Do not apply visual separation or issue VFR or VFR-on-top clearances in Class A airspace.
items.back: 7-1-1: Class A airspace restrictions — three prohibitions.

items.front: Two conditions for clearing aircraft to maintain VFR conditions per 7-1-2
items.back: 1. Pilot on IFR flight plan requests VFR climb/descent. 2. TERMINAL — clearance results in noise-abatement benefit where IFR departure route does not conform to FAA-approved noise-abatement route or altitude.

items.front: MAINTAIN VFR CONDITIONS UNTIL (time or fix)
items.back: 7-1-2 phraseology — VFR conditions with a time or fix limit.
7-1-2 phraseology — VFR climb or descent within a defined altitude block.
7-1-2b phraseology — issued when controller judgment determines VFR conditions may become impractical.

items.front: Four items approach control issues to VFR arriving aircraft per 7-1-3
items.back: a. Wind, runway, altimeter (omit if ATIS code stated or 'have numbers'). b. Traffic info (workload permitting). c. Time/place to contact tower. d. Aircraft may be instructed to contact approach upon initial tower contact. Note: 'have numbers' does not indicate ATIS receipt.

items.front: 'Have numbers' — what does it mean and not mean per 7-1-3?
items.back: It means the pilot has the landing numbers and may support omitting wind/runway/altimeter. It does NOT indicate receipt of the ATIS broadcast.
Paragraphs 7-6-1 (Application) and 7-6-2 (Service Availability) for additional approach control service procedures.

items.front: Visual holding fix requirements for VFR aircraft per 7-1-4
items.back: TERMINAL. Use prominent geographical fixes easily recognized from the air, preferably on sectional charts. Depicted VFR checkpoints are preferred unless pilot shows local familiarity. Issue traffic info to aircraft at same fix.

items.front: HOLD AT (location) UNTIL (time or condition), TRAFFIC (description) HOLDING AT (fix, altitude if known)
items.back: 7-1-4 phraseology — visual holding instruction for VFR aircraft with traffic information.
Depicted VFR checkpoints are preferred UNLESS the pilot exhibits familiarity with the local area — then other prominent geographical fixes may be used.
